$comment: Progression-v2 Racing DNA catalog. Definition ids and versioned effects are immutable once released; later balance changes must append a new definition version. DNA costs no Skill Points, survives resets, and every effect is deterministic from journaled career facts.

definitions.name: The Prodigy
definitions.description: Exceptional results accelerate a young star's reputation and offer momentum, but the paddock permanently judges every failure against a higher ceiling.

persistentEffects.summary: Exceptional results amplify reputation and offer-score movement.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Expectations are harsher and failures lose reputation at the same amplified rate.

definitions.name: The Pole Sitter
definitions.description: Qualifying against expectation is the center of this identity; Saturdays pay more, while Sundays and failed qualifying attempts pay less.

persistentEffects.summary: Qualifying versus expectation contributes additional XP and reputation.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Sunday progression is reduced and qualifying failures are more damaging.

definitions.name: The Engineer
definitions.description: Clean classified finishes turn into development credit for the current team, at the expense of personal fame and portable sponsor momentum.

persistentEffects.summary: A clean classified finish adds development credit for the current team.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Personal fame and sponsor gains are reduced, and banked team affinity is lost on departure.

definitions.name: The Circuit Specialist
definitions.description: One chosen circuit family becomes a permanent specialty; progression rises there and falls everywhere else.

persistentEffects.summary: Rounds in the persisted track family grant enhanced progression.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Rounds outside the persisted track family grant reduced progression.

choice.prompt: Choose the circuit family that defines this driver's specialty.

definitions.name: The Hard Charger
definitions.description: Positions gained and aggressive overperformance pay more, while lost positions, driver-error DNFs, and crash exposure carry larger penalties.

persistentEffects.summary: Positions gained and aggressive overperformance receive enhanced XP and reputation.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Lost positions, driver-error DNFs, and crash injury exposure carry larger penalties.

definitions.name: The Giant Killer
definitions.description: Beating stronger machinery is the prize; equal or superior equipment reduces the value of otherwise similar results.

persistentEffects.summary: Beating stronger team tiers increases OPI, XP, and reputation movement.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Results in equal or superior machinery provide reduced progression.

definitions.name: The Closer
definitions.description: The final third of a season creates extra momentum, but the opening third develops slowly and a late collapse is especially costly.

persistentEffects.summary: Results in the final third of a season receive stronger progression and reputation weighting.

tradeoffEffects.summary: The opening third advances more slowly and a late collapse is penalized more heavily.

definitions.name: The Duelist
definitions.description: A chosen rival becomes the center of the career's competitive story; winning that duel pays heavily and losing it hurts disproportionately.

persistentEffects.summary: Beating the persisted rival is heavily weighted for XP, reputation, and headlines.

tradeoffEffects.summary: The rest of the field matters less and losing to the persisted rival hurts disproportionately.

choice.prompt: Choose the rival whose head-to-head result defines this driver's career.

definitions.name: The Comeback Veteran
definitions.description: Returns from injury, missed races, or unemployment are easier and retain an age-risk offer floor, while uninterrupted careers progress more slowly.

persistentEffects.summary: Recovery after injury, missed races, or unemployment is shorter and return offers retain an age-risk floor.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Continuous uninterrupted seasons generate progression more slowly.

definitions.name: The Survivor
definitions.description: The first serious accident each season is deterministically made less severe, but conservative perception limits elite interest and aggressive-result fame.

persistentEffects.summary: The first serious accident each season is downgraded by one deterministic severity step.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Conservative perception reduces elite-team offer score and aggressive-result fame.

definitions.name: The Iron Athlete
definitions.description: Long-distance events and dense calendars avoid recovery erosion and minor injuries heal faster, while sprint and qualifying achievements pay less.

persistentEffects.summary: Long-distance events and dense calendars avoid recovery erosion, and minor injuries heal faster.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Sprint and qualifying achievements grant less XP and media value.

definitions.name: The All-Rounder
definitions.description: Weather, circuit, and machinery extremes are damped in both directions, creating a dependable identity without specialist peaks.

persistentEffects.summary: Conditional weather, circuit, and machinery penalties are damped.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Conditional specialist bonuses are damped by the same identity ceiling.

definitions.name: The Points Machine
definitions.description: Consecutive classified finishes build a reliability dividend, but one DNF resets the streak and headline-grabbing results carry less commercial value.

persistentEffects.summary: Consecutive classified finishes build a capped XP and contract-patience dividend.

tradeoffEffects.summary: A DNF resets the streak, while wins and poles create less sponsor and headline value.

definitions.name: The Ice Man
definitions.description: Title deciders and high-pressure rounds damp negative movement, while ordinary early and mid-season success advances more slowly.

persistentEffects.summary: Negative form and reputation movement is damped in title deciders and high-pressure rounds.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Ordinary early and mid-season success provides less progression.

definitions.name: The Strategist
definitions.description: A declared season objective shifts value from individual races into relationship and development credit at the campaign level.

persistentEffects.summary: Completing the persisted season objective grants relationship and development credit.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Individual-race XP is reduced and missing the declared objective forfeits the completion dividend.

choice.prompt: Choose the season objective this driver will prioritize.

definitions.name: The Pay Driver
definitions.description: Portable sponsor backing opens otherwise inaccessible offers, while sporting reputation, salary, and team patience remain limited by the stigma.

persistentEffects.summary: Portable sponsor backing opens offer shortlists and can bypass a normal reputation floor.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Salary, sporting reputation, and team patience are reduced by the pay-driver stigma.

definitions.name: The Privateer
definitions.description: Independent racing retains more income and reduces operating costs, while works-team interest and factory development are harder to earn.

persistentEffects.summary: Start and prize income retention rises while independent operating and repair costs fall.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Works-team interest and factory-development benefits are reduced.

definitions.name: The Mercenary
definitions.description: Changing teams pays immediately and avoids adaptation loss, but loyalty, long contracts, and retention offers never develop fully.

persistentEffects.summary: Team switches grant a signing premium and remove the usual first-season adaptation penalty.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Loyalty cannot rise above neutral, and long or retention contracts are weaker.

definitions.name: The Rain Master
definitions.description: Wet-round overperformance receives a major progression premium, while dry-round results provide reduced growth.

persistentEffects.summary: Wet-round overperformance grants strongly enhanced XP, OPI, and reputation.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Dry-round progression is reduced, especially when merely meeting expectation.

definitions.name: The Storm Chaser
definitions.description: Mixed and changing conditions reward successful adaptation, while completely stable conditions reduce progression.

persistentEffects.summary: Mixed or changing conditions damp penalties and reward successful adaptation.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Fully stable wet or dry rounds provide reduced progression.

definitions.name: The Sunshine Specialist
definitions.description: Dry and hot results earn extra progression and sponsor value, while wet results receive a harsher expectation and reputation penalty.

persistentEffects.summary: Dry or hot qualifying and race results earn enhanced progression and sponsor value.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Wet results carry a larger expectation and reputation penalty.

definitions.name: The Journeyman
definitions.description: Lower-tier teams, unfamiliar classes, and emergency vacancies are easier to access, while elite salary and celebrity ceilings remain lower.

persistentEffects.summary: Lower-tier teams, unfamiliar classes, and emergency vacancies use a reduced reputation floor for one-year offers.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Top-team salary, celebrity growth, and long-term elite interest are reduced.

definitions.name: The Loyalist
definitions.description: Consecutive seasons with one team build relationship, development, and patience, while changing teams erases the stack and adds an adaptation penalty.

persistentEffects.summary: Each consecutive season with one team stacks relationship, development, and contract patience.

tradeoffEffects.summary: A team change erases the stack and imposes a first-season relationship penalty.

definitions.name: The Team Leader
definitions.description: Constructor objectives and teammate success become part of the driver's reputation, while individual glory and win-focused commercial rewards are reduced.

persistentEffects.summary: Constructor objectives and teammate success contribute to reputation, relationship, and development.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Individual glory, salary bonuses, and win-focused sponsor value are reduced.

definitions.name: The Showman
definitions.description: Comebacks, upsets, and dramatic results generate larger headlines and sponsor movement, while routine points matter less and visible failure swings harder.

persistentEffects.summary: Comebacks, upsets, and dramatic results amplify headlines and sponsor growth.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Routine points pay less and visible failure causes larger reputation swings.

definitions.name: The Quiet Professional
definitions.description: Media, scandal, and sponsor-health volatility is reduced and team patience improves, at the cost of celebrity and breakout upside.

persistentEffects.summary: Media, scandal, and sponsor-health movement is damped while team patience improves.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Celebrity ceiling, sponsor upside, and breakout-weekend gains are lower.

definitions.name: The National Hero
definitions.description: Home-country races, teams, and compatible sponsors generate extra reputation and commercial value, while foreign opportunities apply a modest penalty.

persistentEffects.summary: Home-country races, teams, and compatible sponsors enhance reputation and commercial value.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Foreign teams and sponsors apply a modest offer-score penalty.

choice.prompt: Choose the nationality affinity that defines this driver's home audience.

definitions.name: The Late Bloomer
definitions.description: Early progression is deliberately slower, but later development accelerates and age-risk onset is deferred.

persistentEffects.summary: Later-career XP accelerates and age-risk onset is deferred.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Early-career XP is reduced and the opening career develops more slowly.

definitions.name: The Old School Racer
definitions.description: Mechanical failures in unreliable machinery harm reputation less and dangerous-era teams value resilience, while modern commercial and technical teams are less interested.

persistentEffects.summary: Mechanical DNFs in reliability-poor machinery damage reputation less and dangerous-era teams value resilience.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Modern commercial, media, and technology-led teams score the driver less favorably.

definitions.name: The Era Chameleon
definitions.description: The first season after a class or era transition avoids unfamiliarity and grants adaptation XP, while repeated seasons in unchanged machinery build mastery and loyalty more slowly.

persistentEffects.summary: The first season after a class or era transition avoids unfamiliarity penalties and grants adaptation XP.

tradeoffEffects.summary: Repeated seasons in unchanged machinery build mastery and loyalty more slowly.
